assertions.description: SharedWorker is exposed in window context — the constructor the extendedLifetime option attaches to.
A SharedWorker instance exposes a .port MessagePort for client communication.
assertions.test: (()=>{try{const blob=new Blob(['onconnect=e=>{e.ports[0].postMessage(1);};'],{type:'application/javascript'});const url=URL.createObjectURL(blob);const w=new SharedWorker(url);const ok=w.port instanceof MessagePort;w.port.close();URL.revokeObjectURL(url);return ok;}catch(e){return false;}})()

assertions.description: The SharedWorker constructor accepts the extendedLifetime: true option without throwing.
The pre-existing name option for SharedWorker still works — the new extendedLifetime option is additive.
SharedWorker still accepts {type: 'module'} for ES module workers — the option dictionary remains extensible.
